meta.siteDescription: A MEXT-accredited Japanese language school operated by Hirai Gakuen, located in Sagamihara, Kanagawa. We support international students from Japanese language study through vocational-school admission to careers in Japan.

common.schoolName: Kanagawa College of Judo Therapy and Acupuncture
common.address: 6-7 Matsugae-cho (Hirai Gakuen Kanagawa Judo Therapy College, Building 3), Minami-ku, Sagamihara, Kanagawa 252-0313, JAPAN

noticeBar.info: Operated by School Corporation Hirai Gakuen  ·  Opening October 2026

hero.sub: Based in Sagamihara, Kanagawa, our Japanese Language Department prepares you for vocational-school admission and a career in Japan. As a MEXT-accredited school under a school corporation with 20+ years of history, we support each student's next step with care.

strongPoints.label: STRONG POINTS
strongPoints.title: Four reasons students choose us.
strongPoints.desc: We do more than teach Japanese. From physical and mental wellbeing to career success and personal growth, we walk with every student.

items.title: MEXT-Accredited
items.desc: As a nationally accredited Japanese language institution, we offer a recognized curriculum and operational standard — a trusted pathway into Japan.

items.title: 20+ Years as a School Corporation
items.desc: Hirai Gakuen, our parent school corporation, was founded in 2001. We bring two decades of educational expertise from judo therapy and acupuncture into language education.

items.title: On-Campus Clinic and Acupuncture
items.desc: A school doctor on staff and on-campus clinics for treatment. Our unique health-focused environment is something no other Japanese language school offers.

items.title: English and Nepali Staff On-Site
items.desc: Mother-tongue support from admission through graduation. We accompany you for housing, banking, healthcare, and every part of daily life.

programs.label: PROGRAMS
programs.title: Three pathways forward.
programs.desc: Entry in April, July, or October — three admission cycles per year. Choose the program that matches your Japanese level and goals. All programs target Japanese Proficiency Level B2 by graduation.

education.label: EDUCATION PHILOSOPHY
education.title: Language × Health × Self-Realization. Three pillars of our education.
education.intro: Through Japanese language education, students gain the linguistic ability and cultural understanding needed for vocational-school studies. At the same time, we cultivate the mental and physical well-being and daily-life habits needed to thrive in Japan, ultimately supporting each student's future career.

items.title: ① Japanese Language for Vocational Study
items.desc: Target: CEFR-J B2 level. We strengthen all five skills — listening, reading, speaking, writing, and interaction — so you can fully engage in Japanese-language vocational classes.

items.title: ② Health and Daily-Life Adaptation
items.desc: School doctor on staff and on-campus clinics. Understanding Japanese culture and customs, and managing health through food — both mind and body are cared for during your study.

items.title: ③ Self-Realization
items.desc: One-on-one counseling, motivation-letter review, and interview practice. Personalized career guidance based on each student's ability and aptitude raises vocational-school admission rates.

items.title: ④ A Multicultural Campus
items.desc: Homeroom-teacher system plus mother-tongue (English / Nepali) supplementary classes. No student is ever left alone, in class or in life.

lifeSupport.label: STUDENT LIFE SUPPORT
lifeSupport.title: Not just learning — life, fully supported.
lifeSupport.desc: New to Japan? You won't be lost. From housing and work to hospitals, banking, and paperwork, mother-tongue staff are with you every step.

items.title: Housing Support
items.desc: Partnered with Able real estate (Odakyū-Sagamihara branch). Nepali- and English-speaking staff assist with everything from apartment hunting to signing the contract. Wide selection of student-friendly properties.

items.title: Health Support
items.desc: School doctor on staff and on-campus clinics for acupuncture and physical therapy. In emergencies, staff accompany you to nearby Kurokouchi Hospital.

items.title: Life Orientation
items.desc: Three sessions per year covering everything you need: residence registration, bank accounts, garbage rules, traffic rules, emergency response, and more — explained carefully.

items.title: Part-Time Work Support
items.desc: Help with the part-time work permit (Shikaku-gai Katsudō Kyoka). Introductions to legal part-time jobs (under 28 hrs/week), résumé writing, and interview practice.

items.title: One-on-One Counseling (4×/year)
items.desc: Four 15-minute life-counseling sessions plus 2 hours of learning-goal counseling each year. Health, worries, work — we talk it all through with each student.

items.title: Mother-Tongue Supplementary Class
items.desc: Supplementary classes in English or Nepali, especially right after enrollment. A 30-minute weekly review session helps you keep pace with the main curriculum.

career.label: CAREER PATHS
career.title: Where our graduates go.
career.desc: Primarily vocational schools in Kanagawa and Tokyo — across medical, welfare, business, IT, culinary, and many more fields. We open the door to a wide range of careers in Japan.

flow.label: ADMISSION FLOW
flow.title: From application to arrival.
flow.desc: Plan ahead — starting around 8 months before your intended entry date.

gallery.label: CAMPUS GALLERY
gallery.title: Life on campus.
gallery.desc: Entrance ceremonies, fieldwork, classrooms, the streets of Sagamihara — a glimpse of our everyday.

captions.ceremony: Entrance ceremony — together with classmates from across the world

access.label: ACCESS
access.title: 7 minutes' walk from Odakyū-Sagamihara Station.
access.desc: A quiet residential area, just 45 minutes from central Tokyo. Affordable rent and daily costs make it one of the most livable areas for international students.

cta.desc: Request a brochure or get in touch any time. Inquiries welcomed in 中文, English, or नेपाली.

campusLife.title: Campus Life
campusLife.subtitle: Beyond the classroom — experience Japanese culture and society, in a study-abroad life wrapped in support.
campusLife.annualIntro: Beyond classroom study, we build practical Japanese ability and a global mindset through events and experiences that bring students into Japanese society and culture.

seasons.title: Spring
items: Fieldwork — Oshino Hakkai (Mt. Fuji spring water tour)

seasons.title: Winter
items: Hatsumōde & Kakizome (New Year shrine visit & first calligraphy)

campusLife.communityIntro: Not closed off — we connect closely with the local Sagamihara community, building your ability to live as a member of society.
community: Visits to welfare, medical, and public facilities
campusLife.supportIntro: From mother-tongue support to the homeroom-teacher system — a dedicated support structure for every student.

support.title: Life Orientation
support.desc: 60 minutes, three times per year. Residence registration, bank accounts, garbage rules, traffic rules, emergency response, part-time work rules, and more.

support.title: Individual Life Counseling
support.desc: 15 minutes, four times per year. Health check-ins, worries, part-time work status.

support.title: Individual Learning Counseling
support.desc: Two hours per student per year. Learning goals, progress, achievement, and self-evaluation — heard and advised on.

support.title: Mother-Tongue Supplementary Class
support.desc: In English or Nepali. Eases first-day anxiety and helps late-arriving students catch up.

support.title: Weekly Tutorial
support.desc: 30 minutes weekly. For students who score below 60% on quizzes/midterms three times in a row.

support.title: Medical Support
support.desc: School doctor on staff. On-site clinic and acupuncture facility. In emergencies, staff accompany you to Kurokouchi Hospital.

campusLife.facilitiesIntro: A focused learning environment with comprehensive medical facilities.

facilities.name: 3 General Classrooms
facilities.desc: Flexible layout for pair and group work

facilities.name: Health Room
facilities.desc: First-response space for when you're not feeling well

facilities.name: On-Site Clinic & Acupuncture
facilities.desc: 1st floor of the campus. Treatment by the school doctor available

facilities.name: Bike Parking
facilities.desc: For students who bike to school

programsPage.title: Programs & Curriculum
programsPage.subtitle: All programs target CEFR-J B2 by graduation. The curriculum advances step-by-step from A1, with each level taught in 10-week units.
programsPage.structureIntro: Two daily sessions — Morning (09:00–12:30) and Afternoon (13:00–16:30). Total capacity 100 (5 classes).
programsPage.levelsIntro: Progressive advancement from A1. Each level: 10 weeks × 20 hours/week. All programs target B2.

head: Level
Stage
Weeks
Hours/Week
Learning Outcomes
rows: Handle basic daily-life information and engage in basic communication, reading, and writing.
Communicate and read/write sufficiently to meet daily-life needs.
Develop communication skills to handle everyday problems.
Communicate while citing evidence and reasons.
Organize information to express opinions and discuss problem-solving.
Understand abstract topics with some effort. Acquire basic presentation skills.
Understand a wide range of topics; cite or refute others' views to strengthen own arguments.
Communicate respectfully across cultures; ready for vocational-school lectures.

approach.title: ① Task-First Learning
approach.desc: Tackle a task first, identify what you can and can't yet do, then build the language knowledge. Practical Japanese you can use right away.

approach.title: ② Project-Based Learning (PBL)
approach.desc: In the final term of Year 2, students split into 'Medical/Welfare Track' and 'General Track' — conducting professional interviews and public-facility visits.

approach.title: ③ Pair & Group Work
approach.desc: Lots of opportunity to speak and to listen. Interaction-centered classroom design.

approach.title: ④ All Five Skills, Balanced
approach.desc: Systematic development of listening, reading, speaking, writing, and interaction.

subjects.name: Upper-Beginner Kanji & Vocabulary
subjects.goal: Read and write common kanji and vocabulary. Research kanji seen around school and town, then present.

subjects.name: Intermediate Integrated Japanese
subjects.goal: Express opinions backed by reasons and evidence. Topics: emergencies, local area, time management.

subjects.name: Japanese Culture & Society
subjects.goal: Etiquette in public facilities, cultural comparison with home country. Multi-cuisine restaurant visits and factory tours.

subjects.name: JLPT Preparation
subjects.goal: JLPT N2 grammar, vocabulary, reading, listening. Inferring test-question intent.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.goal: Split into Medical/Welfare or General track; conduct professional interviews and public-facility field research.

programsPage.subjectsNote: Representative examples. The full curriculum combines Integrated Japanese, Kanji & Vocabulary, Listening, Reading, Conversation, Grammar, Japanese Culture, JLPT Prep, and Project Work.

contact.subtitle: Request a brochure, ask about admissions, or inquire about your application — we're happy to hear from you.
contact.langNote: Inquiries are welcome in 中文, English, or नेपाली. Please let us know your preferred language.

careers.title: Careers — Join Our Team
careers.subtitle: We are hiring faculty and staff to launch the Japanese Language Department in October 2026.

whyItems.title: Stable Operation by an Established School Corporation
whyItems.desc: Backed by School Corporation Hirai Gakuen with over 20 years of educational history. MEXT-accredited — a stable workplace for the long term.

whyItems.title: Multicultural Workplace

whyItems.title: Build a Brand-New Department Together

whyItems.title: Convenient Access

Basic English, Chinese, or Nepali communication skills welcome

Experience supporting international students a plus
Authorized immigration application proxy qualification a plus

positions.name: Administrative Staff

English, Chinese, or Nepali communication skills welcome

Weekends, public holidays, summer break, New Year break, paid leave
Full social insurance, full commuting allowance, health checkups, training programs

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about admissions, fees, visas, and life in Japan. If you don't see your question here, please contact us.

items.q: When can I start my application?
items.a: We recommend starting about 8 months before your intended entry. For April entry, that's around the previous August; for July, the previous November; for October, the previous February.

items.q: Can I apply with no prior Japanese study?
items.a: The 2-Year Program starts at A1.1 (with 150+ hours of prior Japanese study). We recommend basic Japanese preparation beforehand — feel free to ask us for guidance.

items.q: What is the selection process?
items.a: Three stages: (1) document review, (2) Japanese written exam, (3) interview (in person or online). See the Admissions page for full details.

items.q: Can I apply through an agency, or do I apply directly?
items.a: We currently have no partner agencies. Please apply directly to our office. If you need help preparing your application, feel free to reach out via the inquiry form.

items.q: What is the total tuition?
items.a: Full-duration totals: 2-Year ¥1,722,000 / 1-Year-9-Month ¥1,530,250 / 1-Year-6-Month ¥1,334,500 (includes exam and admission fees). See the Admissions or Disclosure page for details.

items.q: Are there other costs besides tuition?
items.a: Living costs (rent, food, utilities, national health insurance) are about ¥150,000/month. Initial housing costs are separate; textbooks and activity fees are already in the tuition.

items.q: How can I pay?
items.a: Three options: (1) bank transfer (Johnan Shinkin Bank), (2) QR code payment (NewAge), or (3) Flywire (lower fees, supports overseas cards).

items.q: What if my visa is denied — do I get a refund?
items.a: If your visa is denied, the exam fee and admission fee are non-refundable, but tuition and other fees are refunded in full (minus wire fees). See the Admissions page for the full refund policy.

items.q: Are scholarships available?
items.a: We do not currently offer our own scholarship. Government and external scholarships (MEXT, JASSO, etc.) will be introduced individually after enrollment.

items.q: How is the COE (Certificate of Eligibility) handled?
items.a: After acceptance, the School applies for your COE on your behalf. You only need to submit the required documents.

items.q: Can I work part-time?
items.a: Yes — with a Shikaku-gai Katsudō Kyoka (part-time work permit), up to 28 hrs/week during term and 8 hrs/day during long breaks. Adult entertainment industries are not allowed. We help you apply and explain the rules.

items.q: What about visa renewal?
items.a: When renewal time comes, our office supports the process. Attendance and grades affect the review — please take your studies seriously.

items.q: What is the daily schedule?
items.a: 4 hours/day (four 45-minute periods). Morning session 9:00–12:30 / Afternoon session 13:00–16:30. Five days/week (no weekends/holidays), 20 hours per week.

items.q: What if I fall behind?
items.a: We offer supplementary classes in English or Nepali, a 30-min weekly tutorial, and 2 hours/year of one-on-one academic counseling.

items.q: Is there JLPT preparation?
items.a: Yes. In Year 2 we run JLPT N2 preparation classes — covering grammar, vocabulary, reading, and listening.

items.q: How does career guidance work?
items.a: Homeroom-teacher system plus one-on-one career counseling. School-selection advice, application/motivation-letter review, interview practice (individual and group), and JLPT classes — all the way through to acceptance.

items.q: How is housing arranged?
items.a: We partner with Able (Odakyū-Sagamihara branch). Nepali- and English-speaking staff help you from apartment hunting to signing the contract. Many student-friendly options available.

items.q: What if I get sick or injured?
items.a: A school doctor is on staff, and our on-site clinic and acupuncture facility can treat you. In emergencies, staff accompany you to nearby Kurokouchi Hospital. Students enroll in national health insurance and an international-student plan.

items.q: Can I get support in my native language?
items.a: Yes — English and Nepali-speaking staff are on-site. Residence registration, bank accounts, medical, government paperwork — we walk through everyday life with you.

items.q: What events does the school hold?
items.a: Spring: tea ceremony, Oshino Hakkai trip. Summer: Tanabata festival. Autumn: Kamakura / Enoshima trip. Winter: Christmas party, New Year shrine visit, first calligraphy. Cultural experiences year-round.

items.q: What are the typical career paths?
items.a: Graduates primarily continue to vocational schools in Kanagawa and Tokyo (medical, welfare, business, IT, culinary, design, and more). See About or Disclosure for more on graduate pathways.

items.q: How do I choose a vocational school?
items.a: Through one-on-one counseling we recommend schools based on your ability, aptitude, and goals. We also have referral arrangements with partner schools.

faq.ctaDesc: If your question isn't covered above, please reach out via the inquiry form. Inquiries in 中文, English, and नेपाली are all welcome.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: School Corporation Hirai Gakuen — Kanagawa College of Judo Therapy & Acupuncture, Japanese Language Department (the 'School') handles personal information of prospective students, current students, guardians, and other parties in accordance with applicable laws and guidelines.

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
sections.body: Through application forms and the inquiry form, we collect information needed for our work — including name, address, contact, education and work history, sponsor information, and passport details.

sections.title: 2. Purpose of Use
sections.body: We use this information only for admissions screening, COE applications, student and career guidance, replying to inquiries, and emergency contact.

sections.title: 3. Third-Party Disclosure
sections.body: We do not share personal information with third parties without your consent, except when required by law (e.g. submission to the Immigration Services Agency) or with contracted partners (real estate) on a minimum-necessary basis.

sections.title: 4. Security
sections.body: We take appropriate physical, technical, and organizational measures to prevent unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or leakage of personal information.

sections.title: 5. Disclosure / Correction / Deletion
sections.body: Upon a verified request from the person concerned (or their legal representative), we will respond promptly within a reasonable scope to requests for disclosure, correction, or deletion of personal information.

sections.title: 6. Contact
sections.body: For inquiries about personal information, please contact the School's administrative office.

about.title: About the School
about.subtitle: A locally-rooted vocational school with 20+ years of history — supporting every international student's next step.
about.philosophy: Through Japanese language education, students gain the linguistic ability and cultural understanding needed for vocational-school studies. At the same time, we cultivate the physical and mental well-being and daily-life habits needed to thrive in Japan, ultimately supporting each student's future career.

purposes.title: ① Japanese Language for Vocational Study
items: Reach CEFR-J B2 level, ready to follow classes taught in Japanese.
Build all five skills (listening, reading, speaking, writing, interaction) in balance for academic learning.

purposes.title: ② Raising Health Awareness
items: Lead a healthy study-abroad life and become independent.
Understand cultural and lifestyle differences to live comfortably in Japan.
Maintain health through food, building a balanced lifestyle that cares for body and mind.

purposes.title: ③ Self-Realization
items: Through varied extracurricular experiences, clarify your own future direction.
Discover your abilities and aptitudes, raise learning motivation, and stay actively engaged.
Become an independent member of society, collaborate with others, and unleash your full potential.

goals: Master CEFR-J B2 level Japanese — including logical writing and presentation skills
Cultivate health and daily-life adaptation — understanding Japanese culture, customs, and balanced nutrition
Build a structured admissions-support system — counseling, school selection, interview practice, motivation-letter review
about.featuresTitle: What Makes Us Different — A Medical-Sciences Parent School
about.featuresBody:
Since its founding, Kanagawa College of Judo Therapy & Acupuncture has been a locally-rooted vocational school, training judo therapists and acupuncturists with over 20 years of history. The school includes an on-site clinic and acupuncture facility where local residents receive treatment — giving students real-world observation opportunities.
The area around Odakyū-Sagamihara Station features many clinics and acupuncture practices — a clear sign of the community's strong interest in health and welfare. The fact that most of our graduates find placement at local medical and welfare facilities further demonstrates the bond between school and town.
We bring this educational environment fully into our Japanese Language Department, with a curriculum that emphasizes understanding of and empathy for the local community. By supporting international students in adapting to life in Japan and systematically acquiring the language ability and cultural understanding needed for vocational school, we aim to develop talent capable of contributing to society in medical, welfare, and many other fields.

admissions.title: Admissions
admissions.subtitle: Everything you need to know about applying, from the first step to your arrival in Japan.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
items: Completed 12 years of formal education in your home country
Granted (or able to be granted) permission to enter Japan through proper procedures
Able to cover living expenses while in Japan, or have a guarantor who can
Willing to abide by the Department's regulations
japaneseLevelRows: A1.1 equivalent or 150+ hours of Japanese study

selection.title: Three-Stage Selection
selection.intro: Applicants are evaluated in three stages: document review, written exam, and interview. The Department Head makes the final decision.

stages.name: Document Review
stages.method: Verification of the five eligibility criteria, with strict review of financial capacity.
stages.criteria: Sponsor's identity, financial status, and ability to fund the studies

stages.name: Written Exam (Japanese)
stages.method: Held at a venue arranged by the School (including online).

stages.name: Interview
stages.method: In-person at the local site or online. Both the applicant and the financial sponsor are interviewed.
stages.criteria: Motivation, Japanese and English ability, character, feasibility of financial support — all evaluated holistically

documents.title: Required Documents
documents.intro: Please submit your documents to the School's office by the deadline.
applicant: Official form. Name must match passport exactly
Official form. Fill out full education and work history with no gaps
Original (if currently enrolled in high school/university)
Original (if currently enrolled in high school/university)
Include entry/exit pages if you have visited Japan
4cm × 3cm, taken within 3 months. Write name, nationality, and DOB on the back.

If you want certificates returned, please notify us at the time of application.
Foreign-language documents must include Japanese translations.

Pay the second-year tuition no later than 15 days before your one-year enrollment anniversary.

feesPage.refundIntro: If you must cancel your enrollment due to unavoidable circumstances, refunds follow the policy below.

refundRules.case: Before entry — cancellation after COE application submission
refundRules.rule: Exam fee and admission fee are non-refundable. Tuition refunded (less wire fees) upon return of admission permit.

refundRules.case: Before entry — COE issued but no visa applied
refundRules.rule: Exam fee and admission fee are non-refundable. Tuition refunded upon return of COE and admission permit.
Exam fee and admission fee are non-refundable. Tuition refunded after verification.

refundRules.case: Before entry — cancellation after visa issued
refundRules.rule: Exam fee and admission fee are non-refundable. Tuition refunded less a cancellation fee (max ¥15,000).
Once exit from Japan and visa expiry are confirmed, tuition / activity / insurance / health fees refunded less a ¥15,000 cancellation fee.
Tuition for the term after withdrawal refunded less a cancellation fee (20% or ¥50,000, whichever is lower).

features.title: 7. Characteristics as a Japanese Language Institution
features.intro: Since its founding, Kanagawa College of Judo Therapy & Acupuncture has been a locally-rooted vocational school, training judo therapists and acupuncturists with over 20 years of history. The school includes an on-site clinic and acupuncture facility where local residents receive treatment — providing students with real-world observation opportunities.
features.body: We bring this educational environment fully into our Japanese Language Department, with a curriculum that emphasizes understanding of and empathy for the local community. By supporting international students in adapting to life in Japan and systematically acquiring the language ability and cultural understanding needed for vocational school, we aim to develop talent capable of contributing to society in medical, welfare, and many other fields.

activities.title: 8. Extracurricular Activities
activities.intro: Beyond the classroom, we cultivate practical Japanese ability and a global mindset through events and experiences that bring students into Japanese society and culture.

seasons.title: Spring
seasons.items: 2-Year Program entrance ceremony, fieldwork (Oshino Hakkai), tea ceremony

seasons.title: Summer
seasons.items: 1-Year-9-Month Program entrance ceremony, Tanabata festival, evacuation drill, health checkup, summer break

seasons.title: Winter
seasons.items: Christmas party, Hatsumōde & Kakizome, vocational-school info session by field, welfare facility visit

activities.external: Community partnerships: Sagamihara disaster drills, cleanup volunteering, Sagamihara International Lounge cooperation, visits to welfare/medical/public facilities.

career.title: 9. Graduate Outcomes
career.intro: Our graduates primarily advance to vocational schools in Kanagawa and Tokyo, pursuing careers in medical, welfare, business, IT, culinary, and many other fields.
career.note: * As this is our inaugural year, graduate placement records will be published starting in fiscal year 2027.

selfEvaluation.title: 10. Self-Assessment & Evaluation
selfEvaluation.intro: As an accredited Japanese language institution, we conduct an annual self-assessment across 15 categories and ~110 indicators to continually improve operations and educational quality. Results are published on this site each April.

disclosure.footer: This page is published in accordance with the public-disclosure obligations for accredited Japanese language institutions (Article 15 of the Ministerial Ordinance on the Accreditation of Japanese Language Institutions).
